channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump's Commerce Secretary Pick Brings Cheer to Crypto Sector
item.description: Howard Lutnick's appointment as Commerce secretary brings hope for cryptocurrency adoption, with his enthusiasm and influence likely to shape US policy.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Trump watches as Musk's SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Trump joined Elon Musk on Tuesday in Texas to observe the launch of a SpaceX rocket as the two leaders have spent significant time together since Election Day. Trump was joined by some of his staff and Republican lawmakers, including Sens. Ted Cruz (R-Texas), Bill Hagerty (R-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Amazon and SpaceX are challenging the NLRB’s constitutionality in federal court
item.description: Attorneys for Amazon and Elon Musk’s SpaceX argued in a federal appeals court Monday that the National Labor Relations Board’s structure is unconstitutional, advancing a legal fight that may last into the Trump administration where Musk is expected to oversee bureaucratic cost-cutting.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase
item.description: The Performance Accountability Council latest's report on “Trusted Workforce 2.0 said agencies have updated all major policies related to personnel vetting. The post Overhaul of security clearance and personnel vetting process enters new phase first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: CMA gives Google’s $2B Anthropic investment the green light
item.description: It took the UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) less than a month to decide that there is no need to proceed further with a merger investigation after Google’s purchase of a $2 billion stake in Anthropic. In a statement released on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM Expands AI Accelerator Options with AMD Instinct MI300X
item.description: IBM and AMD partner to bring high-performance accelerators to IBM Cloud, expanding AI options for enterprise users.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Government commission proposes Manhattan Project-style AI funding plan
item.description: A U.S. congressional commission proposed a "Manhattan Project-like" initiative for the funding of artificial intelligence (AI) development in a larger push to stay ahead of China's technological advancements. The report, issued Tuesday by the bipartisan U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: These midsized companies bring fresh thinking to quietly important jobs
item.description: Equipment has always needed maintenance. We’ve been booking travel online for years. Even using speech recognition to create on-screen captions is not exactly a breakthrough new technology.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The 5 next big things in media, entertainment, and social for 2024
item.description: At a time when old-school media is on the decline, innovators are using technology to create more dynamic content experiences.
item.dc:creator: Maia McCann, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Linux command line for beginners: 12 essential commands
item.description: Getting started on the Linux command line might seem overwhelming at first, but the many commands you need to use will fall into place more quickly than you might imagine. Let’s start with some introductory but very important commands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Agentic AI design: An architectural case study
item.description: From obscurity to ubiquity, the rise of large language models (LLMs) is a testament to rapid technological advancement. Just a few short years ago, models like GPT-1 (2018) and GPT-2 (2019) barely registered a blip on anyone’s tech radar.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Los CEO cortos de mira abocan a los CIO a lidiar con una deuda tecnológica cada vez mayor
item.description: Muchas organizaciones están hipotecando su futuro reasignando recursos de TI de proyectos a largo plazo para lograr objetivos a corto plazo, según una encuesta reciente a directores ejecutivos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ seeks historic action against Google, proposes force-sale of Chrome
item.description: The US Department of Justice (DOJ) is intensifying its antitrust actions against Google, proposing a historic move that could reshape the tech landscape.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Luz verde al nuevo laboratorio de innovación en APIs para Open Gateway
item.description: MasOrange, Telefónica, Vodafone Global y el centro de investigación i2CAT se han unido para poner en marcha el primer laboratorio de APIs Open Gateway multioperador de Europa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: IFCO Launches Global Digital Hub in Barcelona, Reinforcing Commitment to Sustainability and Innovation
item.description: IFCO, a leading provider of reusable containers for fresh food, marks a new milestone with the launch of its global digital hub in Barcelona. The facility aims to enhance customer experience through real-time tracking and data-driven insights, promoting transparency, efficiency, and sustainability across the supply chain.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Succeeding with observability in the cloud
item.description: In its 2024 report, Splunk breaks down observability practices into four stages: foundational visibility, guided insights, proactive response, and unified workflows. Based on that, it classifies companies into four stages of observability maturity: beginning, emerging, evolving, and leading.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 32 state attorneys general call on Congress to pass Kids Online Safety Act
item.description: A bipartisan coalition of more than 30 attorney generals is urging Congress to pass the Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA) this year, stating the legislation will “establish better safeguards for minors online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Akka distributed computing platform adds Java SDK
item.description: Akka, formerly known as Lightbend, has released Akka 3, an update to the JVM-based distributed computing platform that adds a Java SDK as well as serverless and “bring your own cloud” deployment options. Akka 3 and the company’s name change were announced November 15.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump appears to be planning to attend SpaceX ‘Starship’ launch scheduled for Tuesday in Texas
item.description: Trump’s visit comes as billionaire Musk has been a near-constant presence at Trump’s side as he builds out his administration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational
item.description: The ETAC has quickly scaled from a pilot project to an operational center at the Energy Department for analyzing cyber threats like Volt Typhoon. The post Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helpful hack: Customize your home screen by adding widgets on your iPhone
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows you how to add, customize and manage widgets for quick access to apps and info from your iPhone home screen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 14 great preprocessors for developers who love to code
item.description: As much as we love them, programming languages can often feel like straitjackets. They’re a complex bundle of syntactic rules, and if we break them—even once—the compiler starts screaming out error messages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI chatbot reportedly says to eat deadly mushrooms
item.description: “FungiFriend” was added to a popular mushroom discussion Facebook group and encouraged members to “sauté in butter” a potentially dangerous mushroom.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Go language evolving for future hardware, AI workloads
item.description: The Go programming language having just turned 15 years old on November 10, proponents now are planning to adapt the Go language to large multicore systems, the latest vector and matrix hardware instructions, and the needs of AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI, cybersecurity drive IT investments and lead skill shortages for 2025
item.description: AI and cybersecurity are driving enterprise tech investments and at the same time exposing the talent gaps that IT teams face.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Understanding Hyperlight, Microsoft’s minimal VM manager
item.description: Virtualization seemed to be one of those technologies that was done. With Hyper-V, VMware, and KVM all offering similar features and performance, all that was left to do was to support new chipset instructions and fix bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Inside the awe-inspiring 'Aurora' supercomputer at Argonne National Lab
item.description: Argonne National Laboratory's Aurora supercomputer is the fastest in the world, capable of a quintillion calculations per second, and is used to model everything from the structure of the universe to the structure of proteins essential to life.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation
